mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so sustained policy cuts should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is invalid if rate cuts coincide with broad risk-off selling rather than valuation support.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are negative in most periods when FEDFUNDS is declining on a 3-month basis, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An upward crossing of FEDFUNDS above a restrictive threshold marks a tightening shock that raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should weaken when inflation is already falling and policy is broadly anticipated.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign flips positive after controlling for recent CPIAUCSL inflation change.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and slower demand, which should depress corporate sales expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when unemployment rises only because labor supply expands without demand deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative in triggered unemployment-upturn windows, or if PAYEMS is stable/rising while the signal remains strongly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS contracts while UNRATE rises, the labor market deterioration is broad-based rather than noise, and equity prices should weaken over the following month as earnings expectations are revised down. This interaction should outperform either labor series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-down and UNRATE-up condition does not produce lower 20-day SPX returns than either component alone, or if interaction Rank IC remains below 0.03 for 3 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and revenue momentum, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next week through improved near-term sales expectations. The effect should be absent when the sales gain is offset by rising unemployment or restrictive policy.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly SPX returns are not positive on average after months with rising RSAFS, or if the signal disappears once UNRATE is held constant.

mechanism_narrative: A large jump in housing starts after a prior slump signals a cyclical demand rebound that usually transmits to cyclicals and broader risk sentiment, supporting S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when housing is coming off a depressed base.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises >= 10% from its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX forward returns are not higher in housing-rebound episodes than in non-triggered months, or if the effect vanishes outside low-base recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO strengthens while CPIAUCSL stays muted, the economy is expanding through real output rather than inflation pressure, which should be equity-positive over the next month because margins and growth expectations improve together. The signal fails if industrial gains are accompanied by accelerating inflation that forces tighter policy.
failure_condition: Reject if combined INDPRO-up and CPIAUCSL-flat conditions do not beat INDPRO alone, or if returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above its 6-month trend.

mechanism_narrative: A declining INDPRO together with flat-or-rising CPIAUCSL indicates stagflationary supply stress, which should hurt S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through margin compression and a higher policy-risk premium. The signal is invalid when output weakness is purely transitory and inflation also falls quickly.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months while CPIAUCSL is flat or rising
failure_condition: Reject if triggered stagflation windows do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in rolling 3-year tests.
